articles.title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
articles.description:
The suspect is alleged to have robbed multiple victims at gunpoint in Dorchester on the afternoon of Jan. 22.

The post Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston police are investigating an armed robbery incident in Dorchester on January 22.
Multiple victims were robbed at gunpoint, raising safety concerns in the community.
Residents are urged to report any information to assist in apprehending the suspect.

articles.title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
articles.description:
“It’s not acceptable for us,” Foxborough Select Board Chair Bill Yukna told lawyers for the Boston hosting committee Tuesday night.

The post No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Foxborough Select Board Chair Bill Yukna demands upfront public safety funding for World Cup games.
No license has been issued yet for the 2026 World Cup events in Foxborough.
Delay in licensing could affect local businesses and public safety preparations for the event.

articles.title: Whose airstrike hit a girls school in Iran? The US says it’s still investigating.
articles.description:
The strike killed at least 175 people, most of whom were likely children, according to state media and health officials.

The post Whose airstrike hit a girls school in Iran? The US says it’s still investigating. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: US investigates airstrike that killed at least 175 people, mostly children, in Iran.
The strike targeted a girls school, raising concerns about civilian safety.
Boston residents may face increased discussions on international military actions and their consequences.

articles.title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
articles.description:
Thursday’s event will give Bostonians a chance to tell Wu directly what they think is and isn’t working about the city.

The post Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mayor Michelle Wu will host her first office hours on Thursday for direct resident feedback.
Residents can share their thoughts on city issues directly with Wu during the event.
This initiative aims to enhance community engagement and address local concerns effectively.
